title: erysimum canescens subsp. rhaeticum
intro: Erysimum canescens subsp. rhaeticum, also known as Rhaetic Wallflower, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which have been attributed to its alkaloid content. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, soothing digestive issues, and exhibiting antioxidant activity. Erysimum canescens subsp. rhaeticum is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
